problems.title: Balanced Brackets
problems.statement: Given a string containing only ( ) [ ] { }, determine whether every bracket is properly opened and closed in the right order.
constraints: The string contains only bracket characters.

problems.title: Primes in Range
problems.statement: List all prime numbers between two bounds, inclusive.
constraints: If there are none, print nothing.
problems.starterCode:
low, high = map(int, input().split())
# print the primes in [low, high], space-separated

problems.title: Spiral Print
problems.statement: Given an n×n grid, print its elements in clockwise spiral order starting from the top-left, as a single space-separated line.
constraints: Exact spacing doesn't matter — we check the numbers in order.
problems.starterCode:
n = int(input())
grid = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(n)]
# print the elements in spiral order

problems.title: Maze Reachable
problems.statement: You're given an n×n grid. # is a wall, . is open floor, S is the start, E is the exit. Determine whether there's a path from S to E moving only up/down/left/right through open floor.
constraints: Exactly one S and one E appear in the grid.
Hint: this is a plain BFS/DFS flood-fill from S — no backtracking needed.
problems.starterCode:
n = int(input())
grid = [input() for _ in range(n)]
# flood-fill from S and check whether E is reached; print "Reachable" or "Not Reachable"
